title: Ceropegia nevillei
taxonomy: Ceropegia nevillei is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is a member of the genus Ceropegia, which includes over 100 species of flowering plants. The Apocynaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants, often referred to as the dogbane or oleander family. This family includes both annual and perennial plants, as well as trees and shrubs. Ceropegia nevillei, as a member of the Apocynaceae family, is a flowering plant that is native to the tropical regions of the world.

intro: Ceropegia nevillei, also known as the Neville's rope plant or the rope vine, is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family. This plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its potential in treating various health conditions such as arthritis and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Ceropegia nevillei include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which have been isolated and studied for their medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, Ceropegia nevillei is commonly prepared as a decoction, extract, or powder, and is also used in the form of oil infusions and topical creams for its therapeutic benefits.
